description_long: This object is a classic biplane with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked one above the other, connected by struts and wires. The fuselage is cylindrical and tapers toward the tail, with a prominent propeller mounted at the nose. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a smaller tailwheel, arranged in a taildragger configuration. The cockpit is positioned just behind the upper wing, likely open, and appears to accommodate a single pilot. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a standard tail assembly. The overall silhouette and structure suggest a vintage or early 20th-century design, commonly seen in urban airshows or as a display piece.
description: A vintage biplane with stacked wings, a front-mounted propeller, and a taildragger landing gear. The cockpit is open and located behind the upper wing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, central propeller, and symmetrical wing struts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical fuselage, open cockpit, and taildragger landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly with vertical and horizontal stabilizers, and the biplane's wing structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the tapering fuselage, cockpit placement, and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted, likely single-seat.
